shape_semantics: cmp=W, vp=L, vpc=_, naive=L -> cmplog (I2S) resolves; value_profile AND naive block; value_profile_cmplog non-decisive. i2s-PRO where the value_profile GRADIENT does NOT help: the resolving move is exact-literal substitution / derived-operand construction / structural-tag assembly, not Hamming-gradient climbing. The G1 discriminator vs the vp-gradient families is that value_profile, with full CMP_MAP feedback, still BLOCKS here.

hypotheses.label: cmplog plants an exact fixed-offset literal/magic constant the VP gradient cannot climb to

params.note: fixed-offset VALUE gate; target byte(s) = the intercepted literal at the gate offset, decoy = a blocking-seed value at the same offset

per_branch_gate.curl_152: ASCII 'Content-Type:' literal (43 6f 6e 74 65 6e 74 2d 54 79 70 65 3a) at the header offset
per_branch_gate.harfbuzz_5494: format field in {1,2,3} plus 'MATH' FOURCC tag

hypotheses.prediction: cmplog's saved corpus is enriched in the TARGET literal byte(s) at the gate offset relative to naive (signed_target_enrich > 0), because I2SRandReplace plants the intercepted constant. A pure value_profile gradient mechanism would instead drive the byte toward the target by Hamming distance and would show no cmplog-specific literal enrichment; the shape already records that value_profile BLOCKS (it is an L), so the literal is NOT gradient-reachable. This rules out the vp-gradient alternative: if the operand were gradient-climbable, value_profile would resolve (be a W) and naive's corpus would drift toward the target rather than staying at decoy values.
hypotheses.g2_independence: operand_enrichment compares cmplog-vs-naive CORPUS byte composition at the gate offset; it does not consult the vpc/vp resolve pattern that defined the shape, so it is independent of the label source.

hypotheses.label: cmplog constructs a DERIVED negative/range value across parser-routing bytes (no stored literal)

params.note: NO fixed literal: operand_literal=null, operand_width_bytes=null, byte_signature=unclear. The winning value is a derived signed integer (%d arg < 0) assembled from multi-field tag/colon/path/numeric bytes, NOT a single intercepted constant at a fixed offset.

hypotheses.prediction: operand_enrichment finds NO fixed-offset target signature for these branches and returns no_gate_signature (per its scope_limits: it skips gates with no fixed literal). The ABSENCE of a clean per-offset literal enrichment, combined with the same i2s-pro shape (cmplog W, vp+naive L), distinguishes derived-operand construction from exact-literal substitution: a stored-literal mechanism would yield a positive signed_target_enrich at a fixed offset, which this branch lacks.
hypotheses.rule: no_gate_signature == true (operand_enrichment returns no fixed-offset literal); hypothesis confirmed by the diagnostic ABSENCE of literal enrichment under the i2s-pro shape, falling to inconclusive if any clean fixed-offset literal IS found (would reclassify to i2s_exact_literal_constant_gate)
hypotheses.g2_independence: the no_gate_signature determination is a property of the gate's byte structure (whether a fixed-offset target literal exists), independent of the resolve pattern.

hypotheses.label: cmplog's resolving corpus drives the upstream table-directory/ScriptList PARSE deep enough to reach and re-enter the script-lookup branch per seed; the blocking corpus aborts the parse early (a structural REACH gate, not a single comparable literal)

params.note: Run cmplog-RESOLVING (W) vs naive-BLOCKING (L) seeds through libafl-<target>-cov; read the branch line per-seed-normalized execution count. The mechanism is STRUCTURAL REACH: a well-formed blob whose table directory + sub-structure successfully parse drives the lookup loop far more per seed than the short near-random blocking fragments, whose malformed header aborts the parse before the structure is ever populated. NOT a single fixed-offset literal (operand_enrichment rule_not_met for such branches) NOR a size+token-count assembly contrast.

hypotheses.prediction: The cmplog-resolving seeds execute the branch line markedly MORE per seed than the naive-blocking seeds (depth_lift >> 1, winner_deeper == true), because their successful parse reaches and iterates the lookup while the malformed blocking fragments never get there. Discriminates structural reach (depth_lift>>1) from single fixed-offset exact-literal (depth_lift ~ 1, both arms hit the line, byte_freq_ratio is the right test) and from size+token assembly (reach is line-execution count, not seed size/token count); also rules out vp-gradient (VP=L cannot Hamming-climb a malformed fragment into a deep parse).
hypotheses.g2_independence: depth_reach measures the per-seed line-execution COUNT each arm's CORPUS drives at the branch line (structural reach), using depth_lift NOT side_lift. It never consults the vp/vpc resolve pattern that defined the shape; the cmplog-vs-naive reach contrast is the campaign-data signal, independent of the analysis-derived label source.

hypotheses.label: cmplog plants the structural TABLE-TAG FOURCC literal (offset-INDEPENDENT) so the table directory parses into a populated lookup structure; the tiny near-random blocking fragments carry the tag nowhere, so the table never parses

params.note: Count the structural table-tag FOURCC literal ANYWHERE in each arm's seed bytes (occurrence-count, NOT fixed-offset byte_freq), so different-sized resolving (well-formed sfnt) vs blocking (6-64B fragment) seeds are handled. The literal is the sfnt table tag the parser routes on, present in resolving seeds at a parse-derived offset and absent from the blocking fragments. NOT the 'dflt' script tag (weakest_evidence_point: not directly visible in resolving seeds) -- use the directly-cited 'GPOS'/'GSUB' table-tag FOURCC, which the analysis byte-diff confirms literally at offset 0x0c in winners.

hypotheses.prediction: The cmplog-RESOLVING seeds contain the table-tag FOURCC literal (winner_literal_count >= 1) while the naive-BLOCKING fragments contain it essentially never (loser_literal_count ~ 0), so literal_presence_ratio << 1. This is a genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism + measurement than the three already-refuted tests on this branch: (a) operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio at a FIXED gate offset) scored rule_not_met because the structural blob places the tag at a parse-DERIVED offset, not a fixed one -- token_count counts the literal at ANY offset, sidestepping exactly that failure; (b) joint_necessity (size+token-count assembly) scored rule_not_met -- this is single-literal PRESENCE/absence, not a structural-token-COUNT >= 2 assembly threshold; (c) depth_reach (per-seed branch-line execution count) scored rule_not_met -- this is corpus byte composition (does the planted FOURCC exist), not line-execution depth. It rules out the vp-gradient alternative: the FOURCC is an exact 4-byte literal value_profile (an L) cannot Hamming-climb to from a fragment that lacks the surrounding table directory, so if it were gradient-reachable value_profile would resolve (be a W). It rules out 'naive just got unlucky on size' (corpus_size_ratio): the discriminator is the SPECIFIC planted table-tag literal, not generic corpus scale.
hypotheses.g2_independence: token_count reads only the resolving-vs-blocking seed byte composition (does the planted table-tag FOURCC occur), never the vp/vpc resolve pattern that defined the shape; the winner-present/loser-absent literal contrast is the campaign-data signal, independent of the analysis-derived label source.

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage 'I2S event' descriptor proposed (I2S is untagged in mutation_op) -- the discriminators are corpus byte-composition enrichment, gate byte-structure, per-seed structural reach depth, and offset-independent literal presence, not lineage events.

No value_profile 'gradient event' descriptor proposed (VP is an invisible CMP_MAP feedback) -- the claim that VP does NOT help rests on the shape's own vp=L plus the operand_kind/reach/literal structure, not on a measured gradient event.

harfbuzz_5562 is a structural gate with different-SIZED resolving vs blocking seeds. The THREE prior tests all scored rule_not_met for the right structural reasons: operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio) fails because the table tag sits at a PARSE-DERIVED, not fixed, offset; joint_necessity (struct_size_and_token_count) fails because the resolving seeds carry too few literal table/script tags to satisfy a count>=2 assembly threshold; depth_reach (per-seed branch-line execution count) fails because the depth lift did not reach 2.0x. This round pivots to token_count -- a genuinely DIFFERENT BUILT tool (bench/tools/token_count.py, in the brief's not-yet-tried pivot list) and a genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism (offset-INDEPENDENT presence of the single planted table-tag FOURCC), NOT a relaxed-threshold re-run of any refuted test. token_count counts the literal ANYWHERE in the seed, so it is immune to the fixed-offset misalignment that sank operand_enrichment.

corpus_size_ratio was considered and REJECTED for harfbuzz_5562: it is a WHOLE-CORPUS, branch-INDEPENDENT cmplog-vs-naive count/entropy comparison (per its own docstring), so it would re-derive a generic homogenization/inflation property of the whole arm, not isolate THIS branch's structural table-tag gate. The branch-specific discriminator is the planted FOURCC literal, which token_count (per-branch resolving/blocking seed sets) measures and corpus_size_ratio cannot.

token_count reads on-disk seed bytes from the resolving_seeds/blocking_seeds DB tables; harfbuzz is on-disk on s4 with 3 cmplog resolving + 20 naive blocking seeds in db/blockers.sqlite for branch 5562, so harfbuzz_5562 IS scorable. operand_enrichment needs an on-disk corpus + >=3 W + >=3 L: SCORABLE only for curl + harfbuzz; bloaty_34 has NO on-disk corpus (bloaty not in {curl,harfbuzz,openthread,sqlite3} on s4 and not on sB's set) so it CANNOT be enrichment-scored on s4 and carries to fallback inconclusive on the measurement even though its mechanism clearly matches the literal hypothesis -- the owning server arbitrates if/when bloaty's corpus is present (cross-server rule).
notes: ROUND-3 (FINAL) re-design. PRIORS KEPT VERBATIM (superset/monotonic, so the 6 confirmed branches re-validate): (1) i2s_exact_literal_constant_gate [operand_enrichment], (2) i2s_derived_signed_operand_construction [operand_enrichment/no_gate_signature], (3) i2s_structural_reach_gate [depth_reach] -- all three retained exactly. NOTE on members: the round-2 hypothesis i2s_structural_reach_gate was the test under which harfbuzz_5562 scored rule_not_met (depth_lift fell below 2.0); per G1 its RULE is kept unchanged (no relaxed-threshold re-run) but harfbuzz_5562 is no longer assigned to it as a member (members:[]), because the depth-reach mechanism did NOT hold for it -- the rule stays in the superset so any future depth-confirmed branch validates, but it is not re-tested on 5562 at a relaxed bar. NEW MECHANISM (this round): i2s_offset_independent_structural_tag_assembly for harfbuzz_5562 -- a genuinely DIFFERENT mechanism from all three refuted tests and from the vp-gradient/corpus-scale alternatives: the I2S-planted table-tag FOURCC ('GPOS'/'GSUB') is PRESENT in the well-formed resolving seeds and ABSENT in the tiny blocking fragments, measured by REUSING the BUILT token_count tool (bench/tools/token_count.py), which counts the literal at ANY offset and so is immune to the fixed-offset misalignment that gave operand_enrichment rule_not_met, the count-threshold that gave joint_necessity rule_not_met, and the per-seed depth that gave depth_reach rule_not_met. Literal = 'GPOS'/'GSUB' (directly byte-diff-cited at 0x0c in winners), NOT 'dflt' (the analysis weakest_evidence_point flags 'dflt' as not directly visible in resolving seeds). G1: discriminates literal-presence from fixed-offset enrichment, from size/token assembly, from per-seed depth, from vp-gradient (a fragment lacking the table directory cannot Hamming-climb to an exact 4-byte FOURCC -> if it could, VP would be a W), and from generic corpus scale (corpus_size_ratio is whole-corpus, branch-independent, rejected here). G3: NO decidable:false sub-group is warranted -- harfbuzz_5562 is on-disk on s4 (3 W + 20 L seeds) and its literal-assembly mechanism IS discriminable by token_count; if the literal turns up in the blocking fragments too (ratio ~ 1) the rule correctly leaves the branch at fallback inconclusive rather than forcing a label. Cross-server: bloaty_34 stays measurement-inconclusive on s4 only because its corpus is absent here, not because the mechanism is unlabelable -- its owning server arbitrates. CAVEAT (carried): the 'vp does not help' claim is asserted from the shape (vp=L) + operand/literal structure, NOT independently measured (VP is an unobservable feedback); this is the honest discrimination ceiling for the WL_L shape, which is why every load-bearing test is a cmplog-vs-naive corpus contrast, never a VP contrast.
